AWS Certification Study Guide

Condensed exam notes — EC2, S3, RDS, VPC, IAM and more. Use the sidebar or press / to search.

Compute›

EC2 – Types Of Pricing

On DemandSpotReservedDedicated Hosts
Pay by the second for Linux instances. Pay by the hour for Windows instances (not the cheapest)Bid on when you want to use compute. Ex. Genomics company client (cheapest)One to Three year contractsPhysical EC2 servers that are dedicated for your use. Ex. Because of regulatory functions or government clients

EC2 Families

DDense storage (File Servers / Data Warehouse / Big Data)
IFast I/O (NoSQL DBs / Data Warehouse)
RRAM Memory Optimized (Memory Intensive / App Servers / DBs)
TLow cost general purpose
MGeneral purpose (Ex. App Servers)
CCompute Optimized (CPU intensive apps / DBs)
GGraphics intensive (Machine Learning, etc.)
FField Programmable Gate Array
PGraphics intensive (Machine Learning, etc.)
XExtreme memory optimized (SAP Hana, Apache Spark)

EBS

  • Block based storage that can be attached to EC2
  • An EBS volume can be attached to only one EC2 instance
SSD General PurposeUpto 10k IOPS. bootable
SSD Provisioned IOPs>10k IOPS upto 20k IOPS. bootable. large relational DBs or NoSQL DBs
HDD Throughput OptimizedLarge amount of sequential data (Big Data / DW / log Processing). cannot be boot volume
HDD ColdLow cost. cannot be boot volume
HDD MagneticSame as above but can be boot volume

Create Test Web Server On EC2

  1. Create EC2 instance through console
  2. Modify security groups to allow SSH / HTTP / HTTPS traffic
  3. Generate private key → store -> chmod 400 private key
  4. ssh ec2-user@ipaddress -I private_key.pem
  5. sudo su → yum update -y (updates instance to latest)
  6. yum install httpd -y [install Apache server]
  7. chkconfig httpd on (auto starts Apache server)
  8. cd /var/www/httpd -> nano index.html → <html><h1>Hello World...
  9. service httpd start → https://ipaddress/index.html
  10. In case of Windows (Putty) use PuttyKeyGen to generate ppk from pem file

EC2 Miscellaneous

  • Termination protection is turned off by default
  • Root EBS volume id deleted when the instance is terminated. Others are not
  • EBS root volumes created from default AMIs cannot be encrypted. However, a copy can be made with encryption applied
  • Additional volumes (non-root) can be encrypted
  • Security Groups: changes take effect immediately. All inbound traffic in blocked by default. Allowing traffic on a particular port is symmetrical i.e. traffic on outbound port is automatically enabled. Outbound traffic is blocked using NACLs
  • EC2 instance metadata: 1) ssh and 2) curl http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data

EBS Miscellaneous

  • Root volumes are where the OS is installed
  • Snapshots of EBS volumes are stored in S3
  • You can create AMIs from both volumes and snapshots
  • You can change volume type / size dynamically
  • EC2 instance and EBS volumes are always on the same AZ
  • To copy to different AZ → copy and then create new volume

RAID Arrays

RAID 0Striped - data split across drives - good perf - no DR - min 2 disks
RAID 1Mirrored - data dup across drives - DR - writes expensive - min 2 disks
RAID 5Min 3 disks - parity information stored in the drive where the data is not being written. This info is used to reconstruct data if any one of the drives fail. Note - RAID5 discouraged on AWS
RAID 10Combination of RAID 0 and RAID 1 – min 4 disks
  • RAIDs apply to Windows EC2 instances. To test we need to RDP into the Windows server → select the EBS drives → Right click and create RAID array
  • Taking snapshots of RAID array is a bit tricky because data is being held in cache and the OS. Solution: stop application from writing to disk+ flush all caches How: 1) freeze file system 2) unmount 3) (preferred) stop associated EC2 instance

AMI Image Creation

  • Select Volume → create snapshot → copy snapshot to different region (option to encrypt at this stage) → create AMI from snapshot → this image can be used to boot additional EC2 instances

Instance Store

  • Ephemeral storage as opposed to EBS
  • Data is lost on stop and terminates. Not lost on reboots.(not the case in EBS)
  • Instance store is directly attached to the EC2 instance

Auto Scaling

  • STEP I : Initial launch configuration
                    →    EC2 instance 1 on AZ 1
ELB      →    EC2 instance 2 on AZ 2
        →    EC2 instance 3 on AZ 3
  • STEP II : Auto scaling setup - Ex. If CPU utilization is greater than 90% for 5 minutes then launch a new instance – min / max can be provided – ramp down configuration can also be provided

EC2 Placement Groups

  • A logical group of EC2 instances within a single AZ for cases when low network latency and high throughput is desired
  • A 10Gbps connection is provided for traffic within the placement group
  • Recommended to have instances of the same type / families within the group
  • Ex. Nodes of a Cassandra cluster that requires low latency / high throughput

Lambda

  • Compute service where you can upload your code and create a Lamda function
  • Truly Serverless - don’t have to worry about provisioning servers / security patches / scaling / etc.
  • Lamda functions can be triggered by API Gateway, AWS IOT, Alexa skills, Alexa SmartHome, CloudFront, CloudWatch Events/Logs, Code Commit, DynamoDB, S3, Kinesis, SNS or another Lamda function
  • Languages supported: C#, Java, Node.js, Python, Go, PowerShell, Ruby
  • Function cannot go beyond 5 minutes. Break down if needed.
  • Pricing based on number of requests and duration of the function

AWS Command Line Interface (CLI)

  • First ssh ec2-user@ipaddress -I private_key.pem
  • If we want to use the CLI command for accessing S3 then there are couple of ways to give EC2 the permission to access S3
Option I
  1. create user w/ programmatic access and S3 access permissions
  2. aws configure → provide credentials
  3. use aws cli commands ex. aws s3 ls or aws s3 help
Option Ii (Preferred)
  1. create a role with S3 access permissions
  2. attach role to EC2 instances through management console
  3. use aws cli commands
  • aws cli command to copy files across regions
    aws s3 cp –recursive s3://test-eu-west-2 /home/ec2-user –region eu-west-2

Bootstrap Scripts

  • During EC2 instance creation: Create Instance → Advanced Detail → In text box enter → #!/bin/bas …and then all the needed commands

Storage›

S3Object based storage – stored in buckets a.k.a folders

  • File sizes can be from 0 to 5TB. Storage is unlimited
  • ‘Read after Write’ consistency for puts (creates) and ‘Eventual’ consistency for overwrites (updates) and deletes
  • What is stored: {key, value, version id, metadata, security info}
  • Pattern: https://s3-us-west-2.amazonaws.com/myBucket/myFile.data

Storage Tiers

S3 – STANDARDS3 (IA)S3 (RRS)
DefinitionStandardInfrequently AccessedReduced Redundancy Storage
Availability99.99%99.99%99.99%
Durability99.999999999%99.999999999%99.99%
NoteLower fee than S3 but fee for retrievalsDurability drops. Ex. for storing thumbnails which can be re-generated if bucket is lost

Glacier

  • Long term storage – multiple hours for retrieval (3-5) – Durability 99.999999999%

S3 Versioning

  • makes copy and stores each copy. watch out for size
  • MFA can be specified to prevent accidental deletes

S3 Lifecycle Management

  • Example: Action on current version - after 30 days → move to S3 IA → after 60 days → move to Glacier → after 1 year expire (same steps for previous versions)

S3 Cross Region Replication

  • Pre-requisite: replication requires versioning to be enables
  • Note: any objects present before enabling of replication are not copied over. This (copy) needs to be done manually. AWS CLI can be used for this purpose
  • Adds/Updates/Deletes are automatically replicated across regions

S3 Security

  • By default, the buckets are not publicly accessible
  • Two options exist for securing the buckets
    Option I (Object Level) – ACLs / unix type / owner - other aws accts - public
    Option II (Bucket Level) – bucket policies
  • Encryption: In-transit SSL/TLS and at-rest server side using SSE-S3, SSE-KMS, SSE-C

S3 Transfer Acceleration

  • S3 TA utilizes the CloudFront edge network to accelerate the uploads to S3
  • You can use a distinct url to upload directly to the edge location which will then transfer to S3 pattern: myBucket.S3-acclerate.amazonaws.com

EFS

  • Elastic File System is a ‘block based’ file storage service for EC2 instances
  • EFS storage capacity is elastic (grows / shrinks automatically) as opposed to EBS volumes where size is pre-determined. Also, no pre-provisioning is required
  • Data is stored across multiple AZs
  • An EFS can be mounted on to 2 or more EC2 instances. main diff w/ EBS
  • After creating EFS instance navigate to EC2 mount instructions to get commands to mount EFS to EC2 instances

Storage Gateway

  • Storage Gateway is a service that connects an on-prem storage appliance with AWS cloud storage mechanisms. This enables integration to be seamless and secure
File Gateway (NFS)Volume Gateway (iSCSI)Tape Gateway (VTL)
Documents / pdfs / pics / audio files / etcStored Volumes: Entire dataset is stored locally as well on AWS Cache Volumes: Frequently accessed data is stored locally and other in cloudVirtual Tape backup

Databases›

Overview

  • Relational: Tables → Table → Row → Column/Value
  • NoSQL: Collections → Collection → Document → Key/Value Pair
OLTP BasedOLTP BasedOLAP Based
RelationalNon Relational / NoSQLData WarehousingCaching Mechanisms
Oracle / SQL ServerMySQL / PostgresMariaDB / AuroraMongoDBCouchDBDynamoDB CassandraCognos / JasperSoftOracle HyperionSAP NetWeaverRedshift / VerticaElastic CacheMemcachedRedis

RDS

  • By default when you create an RDS instance then it is publically available through public ip:3306. You need to modify the security group permissions to ensure that the traffic is allowed only from EC2 instance that hosts the applications

RDS Backups

  • AUTOMATED BACKUPS is ON by default – min 1day max 35days default 7days. Daily snapshot is taken and then the transaction logs are stored through the daya. To get to a particular point in time we go back to the snapshot of the daya and then utilize the transactional logs to get to a particular point in time. DB I/O maybe suspended or will be slow during backups so pick up a time that is slow
  • DATABASE SNAPSHOTS – snapshot that is user initiated

RDS Encryption

  • Can be turned ON only at RDS instance creation time

RDS Multi AZ

  • Backup of PROD instance in a different AZ. use only for DR not for perf

RDS Read Replicas

  • Replica of PROD db but only for reads. max 5. use for read heavy workloads
  • Use for scaling (perf) and not for DR. for DR use Multi AZ
  • Read Replicas at this point are available only within one AZ

DynamoDB

  • Data stored in SSD storage – spread across min 3 AZs by default
  • Use in cases where performance is critical (nope: go relational or cassandra)
  • Eventual Consistent Reads (Default) – Strongly Consistent Reads (i.e. Read after Write) not default
  • Pricing – ‘Provisioned Throughput Capacity’
    Write throughput - $0.0065/hr | 10 units | Read throughput - $0.0065/hr | 50units
    (1 unit = 1 read or 1 write / second for 1 hour)
    Storage - $0.25/GB/month
  • Overall cheaper for READ intensive apps and more expensive for write intensive
  • Automatic push button scaling as opposed to RDS. Super easy and instantaneous . no need to take snapshots and migrate to a new instance (with new config) as is the case when we need to scale RDS instances (i.e. for better cpu, mem, etc)

Redshift

  • Fast and powerful fully managed petabyte scale data warehouse in the cloud
  • Single Node Configuration (160GB) – Multi Node Configuration (Leader Node manages client connections and receives queries | Compute Nodes (max 128 nodes) stores data and performs queries and computations)
  • Data Warehouses: use for data that came copied over from production and then analytics/reports that can be run at a later time (OLAP model)
  • Columnar data storage (OLAP) as opposed to relational which is row based
  • Block size for columnar storage 1024KB/1MB
  • better compression possible – MPP (massively parallel processing) possible

Application Integration›

SQS

  • Simple Queue Service – 256KB max message size
  • Guarantees at at least once delivery – pull based system
  • Messages can be kept for a min of 1 minute or a max of 14 days. Default is 4 days.
  • Two Types – Standard Qs (best effort but order is not guaranteed) and FIFO Qs limited to 300 transactions/second
  • Visibility Timeout: The amount of time the message is sitting invisibly on the queue while it waits for the message to be fully processed. If the job is not fully processed the message becomes visible again after the visibility timeout period. Max = 12 hours
  • There is a risk of duplicate processing here and should be taken care by the app

SWF

  • Simple Workflow Service: Co-ordinate tasks across distributed app components
  • Retention period max time: 1 year
  • Task oriented APIs as opposed to message oriented APIs
  • Message is only ever delivered once – no risk of duplicates
  • SWF Actors – 1) Workflow starters 2) Deciders 3) Activity Workers

SNS

  • Simple Notification Service – Publish Subscribe Mechanism – Multiple Subscribers
  • Send notification and follows a push model rather than a pull
  • Push notifications to mobile devices (Apple, Google, FireOS, etc)
  • Can send notifications through SMS, Email, SQS, Lambda, HTTP, etc.

Management Tools›

CloudWatch / CloudTrail

  • Basic Monitoring 5 mins (free) | Detailed Monitoring 1 minute (paid)
  • Metrics are available by default w/ CloudWatch
    [CPU credit balance, CPU credit usage, CPU utilization]
    [DiskReadBytes, DiskReadOps, DiskWriteBytes, DiskReadOps]
    [NetworkIn, NetworkOut, NetworkPacketsIn, NetworkPacketsOut]
    [StatusCheckFailed, StatusCheckFailed_Instance (EC2 instance is down), StatusCheckFailed_System (hypervisor running EC2 is down)]
  • Note: Memory related metrics are not available by default. Must be added custom
  • ALARMS: ex. if cpu utilization >=80% for 1 period (5mins) → then alarm/email
  • EVENTS: for notification to other AWS services ex. when EC2 instance comes up trigger event that calls Lamda function that updates DNS values in Route53
  • CLOUDWATCH: logs for audit purposes as opposed to monitoring

OpsWorks

  • Configuration Management tool + build/deploy highly dynamic applications
  • OpsWorks Stacks | OpsWorks for Chef Automate (cookbooks/recipes) | OpsWorks for Puppet Enterprise

CloudFormation

  • Templates, scripting, common architecture, few clicks for test env, version control infrastructure architecture/building scripts like code

Developer Tools›

Overview

  • CLOUD9 – IDE in the cloud!
  • CODE COMMIT secure repository in the cloud (what! – like git)
  • CODE BUILD build/test in the cloud (what! like Jenkins)
  • CODE DEPLOY deploy apps to instances in a fast and reliable way (like jenkins)
  • X-RAY deugging/anayze app by tracing, exception collection, profiling (like NewRelic, AppDynamics)

Migration Tools›

Overview

SNOWBALLSNOWBALL EDGE (AWS IN A BOX)SNOWMOBILE (TRUCKS!)
80 TB – import from S3 and export to S3100TB [Storage + Compute]Ex: Monitoring plane flying in artic collecting huge amount of data with some basic processingPetaByte/ExaByte sizeKB → MB → GB → TB → PB → EB (Exa) → ZB (Zetta) → YB (Yotta)

Networking›

Route53

  • IPV4 (32bit) IPV6 (128 bit) – Route53 supports IPV6
  • Top level domain names (.com .net .gov .click etc)
  • Domain name registration example goDaddy
  • Rout53 is also a Domain Name Registrar
  • A Record (Domain DNS name → IpAddress)
  • CNAME Record (Domain DNS Name → Alias Record)
  • ALIAS Record – This is AWS Route53 speak – very similar to CNAME record. One major difference is that naked domain names (apex zone records / ex. http://acloudguru.com) can be mapped using alias records. CNAME records cannot map naked domain names. Note: In Route52 alias record invocations are free. CNAME record invocations are not free
  • TTL (Time to Live) Amount of time DNS lookup is cached in local computer or in local DNS server. In migration type situations lower this value to say 5 minutes. Then wait for a day and then do the migration. Ex. switching from AWS to Azure or vice versa
  • NS Records / SOA Records / Domain Name Server / Domain Name Registrar
  • Route53 Routing Types {Simple/Weighted/Latency/Failover/GeoLocation}
  • SIMPLE: End user → Route53 → London ELB → London EC2
  • WEIGHTED: End user → Route53 → (20%) US East ELB → EC2
                → (20%) US West ELB → EC2
  • LATENCY: End user → Route53 → (check latency) US East ELB → EC2
                          → (check latency) US West ELB → EC2

Check latency and route to appropriate (responsive) ELB

  • FAILOVER: End user → Route53 → Prod ELB → EC2
                         → DR backup ELB → EC2

Forward to backup ELB on failure of primary

  • GEOLOCATION: End user (Europe) → Route53 → EU ELB → EC2
        End user (USA)                           → US ELB → EC2

Route based on origin

  • ELBs do not have public IP Address. Use ALIAS records to map DNS name to ELB alias name

Elastic Load Balancers

  • CLASSIC LOAD BALANCERS: operate at layer 4
  • APPLICATION LOAD BALANCERS operates at layer 7 / preferred / enables inspection of content and routing. i.e. load balancing possible based on the context of the message
  • EC2 instances monitored by ELB are reported as InService or OutOfService
  • Define healthcheck.html and place in /var/www where ELB can ping and detect health in case you want to be more specific
  • ELBs have an assigned DNS name. ELBs are not given public IP addresses. Use Route53 to pass traffic to the ELBs DNS name (Alias Record)
  • When creating ELB configurations you also need to select the subnets across AZs
            ELB → subnet 1 / AZ 1 / EC2
                   → subnet 2 / AZ 2 / EC2

VPC

  • Virtual Data Center in the cloud – Logically isolated section of the AWS cloud where you can launch AWS resources in a virtual network that you define. Enables to have complete control over your virtual network environment including 1) selection of your own IP address ranges 2) creation of subnets and 3) configuration of route tables / gateways to control traffic within the VPC and externally
  • VPC typically consist of private and public subnets. Private subnet – DBs / App Servers / etc. Public Subnet – App Server / Web Server / Service Gateways
  • CIDR.xyz / Amazon supported CIDR ranges
    10.0.0.0 (10/8)
    172.16.0.0 (172.16/12)
    192.168.0.0 (192.168/16)
  • Security groups can span multiple EC2 instances / subnets
  • Each subnet can be part of only one AZ
  • VPC can be part of only one AWS Region
  • Each subnet can be attached to only one NACL
  • Default VPC gets created when we set up an account. All subnets by default, have access to the net
  • Each EC2 instance by default, has a public and private ip address when assigned to the default VPC. However, if attached to a custom VPC then the private subnet wont get a public ip address
  • Max number of VPCs is a region is 5

VPC Peering

  • One VPC connecting / talking to another VPC via a private ip address. Can peer with other VPCs in the same account or with VPCs in another account. Note. There is no transitive peering

VPC Endpoints

  • create VPC endpoints when you want to access AWS service (Example S3) from a private subnet instead of using a proxy NAT Gateway. Note: this applies to accessing an AWS resource from a private subnet and not the internet

VPC Flowlogs

  • capture ip traffic for audit purposes. Traffic generated by Amazon DNS servers + traffic generated from Windows instance looking for license authentication + DHCP traffic are NOT monitored.

VPC Lab

  • CREATE VPC: when you create a new VPC then by default a route table + security group + default NACL are created
  • CREATE SUBNET: create subnet → select VPC → select AZ → select CIDR ranges (IPV6 optional). Note: /24 CIDR ranges is supposed to give 256 but we get only 251 since AWS reserves a bunch
  • CREATE INTERNET GATEWAY: create IGW and pair with VPC. 1 VPC ←→ 1 IGW
  • CREATE ROUTE TABLES: by default, subnets get associated with default route table. We need to create a custom route table that has access to the internet. 1) create new route table 2) create route entry for internal access 3) associate route table with our public subnet. This will enable internet to/from the public subnet
    Note: also check ‘Auto Assign IP Address’ option in the subnet configuration
    Note: route tables enable subnets to talk to each other + reach out to the internet
  • CREATE NEW EC2 INSTANCE: standard steps – configure a new security group when doing this step. 1) For private subnets (DB) {SSH/MYSQL/HTTPS(S)/ALL ICMP}. The ip address range allowed for all of the above should be 10.0.1.0/24 i.e. access only from the public EC2 instance. 2) for public (web/service gateways) {HTTPS(S)/SSH}
  • After having performed above steps then you can access index.html on public EC2 instance. you can ssh into the public EC2 instance and from there you can ping/ssh into the private subnet instance.

NAT Instance / NAT Gateway

  • In the previous example we have internet access for the public subnet / EC2 instance because we configured internet access in the custom route table. The private subnet has no internet IN or OUT. Internet IN should not be allowed but internet OUT is required…example to download and install MySQL. For this activity you need to set up either the NAT Instance (being phased out) or the NAT Gateway (Recommended)
    NAT INSTANCE: Basically, a proxy EC2 instance using which the private EC2 instance in the private subnet can access the internet
    NAT GATEWAY: Recommended approach – Highly available. Managed by AWS. You don’t have to do anything for management. One disadvantage is that NAT Gateways cannot be used as a BASTION server (used to securely administer EC2 instances in private subnets using SSH/RDP)
  • Note: when creating NAT instance ensure the ‘Source/Destination’ check is disabled
  • NAT instances must be in the public subnet
  • more traffic create more powerful NAT instances
  • NAT Gateways can scale upto 10bps
  • Only one NAT gateway per AZ
  • NAT Gateways are more secure than NAT Instances
  • Egress only IG – specific to IPV6

NACLNetwork Access Control List

  • The default NACL that is created allows all traffic IN and OUT (important difference between SGs). Any new custom NACL does not allow any traffic IN or OUT
  • Each subnet can be associated with only one NACL. A single NACL can be associated with multiple subnets
  • NACLs are not symmetric as compared to SGs. The rules created have to be specified for both inbound and outbound traffic separately. NACLs contain a numbered list of rules and they are evaluated in order starting with the lowest #
  • Again…default SG allows nothing. Custom SG allows. Is symmetrical
    default NACL allows everything. Custom NACL doesn’t. Is NOT symmetrical

API Gateway

  • API Management offering for the purpose of PUBLISH / MAINTAIN / MONITOR / SECURE APIs.
  • API Caching: TTL in seconds
  • low cost / scaling super easy / can throttle / can be hooked to CloudWatch for security purposes / CORS can be easily configured and enabled

CloudFront

  • CDN (Content Delivery Network) is a system of distributed servers (network) that delivers static webpages and other web content (audio/video/pictures/etc) to an user based on the geographic location of the user, the origin of the webpage and a content delivery server.
  • EDGE LOCATIONS: this is the location where content is cached. This is independent of the region and AZ
  • ORIGIN: This is the origin of all the files that the CDN will distribute (S3 bucket/EC2instance/ELB/Route53/or custom location)
  • DISTRUBUTION: The CDN name which consists of a collection of edge locations
  • CLOUDFRONT: can be used to deliver your entire webside including dynamic, static, streaming and interactive content using a global network of edge locations. Requests for your content are automatically routed to your nearest edge location so content is delivered with the nest possible performance.
  • CloudFront distribution types: Web distribution (static/dynamic web content) or RTMP (media streaming)
  • Edge locations are not just for reads and can be used for writes as well.
  • There can be multiple origins in the same distribution
  • Caching TTL can be easily configured (86400 is default)
  • You can restrict user access to the CDN URLs by using signed URL cookies
  • Geo Restriction: whitelist (countries where you want to distribute particular content) / blacklist (countries where you do not want to)

DirectConnect

  • AWS DirectConnect makes it easy to establish a dedicated network connection from your from your premises to AWS. Yu can establish a private connection between AWS and your datacenter, office or co-location environment. This reduces network cost, increases throughput and provides a more consistent network experience than internet based connecitons.

Analytics›

Kinesis

  • For handling streaming data (like IOT)
KINESIS STREAMSKINESIS FIREHOSEKINESIS ANALYTICS
consists of shards – stored by default for 24hrs -consumers are fleet of EC2 instances – EC2 instances can inturn send data to DynamoDB, S3, EMR or Redshift – max data retention: 7 daysNo shards – completely automated – data can be analyzed using Lamda – No data retention – can be sent directly to S3, RedShift, etc – No configuration of consumers as opposed to streamsApply sql type queries on the stream data – can be sent to S3, Redshift, ElasticSearch cluster

EMR

  • web service that enables business, researchers, data analysts, developers to easily and effectively process vast amounts of data (big data offering)

Athena

  • interactive query service to analyze large amounts of data in S3

CloudSearch / ElasticSearch

  • CLOUD SEARCH: AWS managed service for search. AWS Elastic Search: service that make it easy to setup, operate and scale an ElasticSearch cluster in the cloud

Security›

IAM

USERSGROUPSPOLICIESROLES
end usersCollection of users under one set of permissionsa.k.a permissionsSecure way to give grant permissions to entities you trust. Ex. app code running on EC2 that needs to access AWS resources or IAM user in another account
  • A policy can be attached to a user or group or a role
  • IAM resources created are available and accessible globally
  • Setup multi factor authentication (MFA) w/ root account [Recommended]
  • Password Mgmt Features present {Rules, Expiry, Recovery, etc}
  • When creating a user OPTION 1 programmatic access {access id/secret}
                   OPTION 2 management console access (usrname/password)

Miscellaneous›

Overview

  • ELASTIC TRANSCODER: pay by minutes you are trans encoding and the resolution
  • SWF Actors {Workflow Starters | Deciders | Activity Workers}
  • Meta Data Query: curl or get http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data. This gives the instance meta data and not the user meta data
  • AWS ORGANIZATIONS and CONSOLIDATED BILLING: Main paying account and associated AWS accounts
  • You get only one monthly bill broken down by sub accounts
  • Cheaper, get discount based on volume – Easy to track charges
  • Best practice: utilize root account just for consolidated billing and don’t utilize for anything else
  • Max – only 20 accounts - Cloud Trail can be applied at root account
  • CROSS ACCOUNT ACCESS: Makes it easier for you to work productively within a multi account AWS environment. Example: think separate account to developers and for production but you want to developers to access S3 buckets for certain logs in production. IN this case you set up cross account access. In management console top right corner you can ‘switch users’
  • CROSS ACCOUNT ACCESS (Steps Involved)
  1. Identify account numbers
  2. Create an IAM group – dev
  3. Create an user in dev
  4. Log into production
  5. Create ‘read-write-app-bucket’ policy
  6. Create ‘UpdateApp’ cross account role
  7. Apply newly created policy and role
  8. Log into developer account
  9. Create a new inline policy
  10. Apply it to the developer group
  11. Log in as ‘John’ i.e. developer user
  12. Switch accounts i.e. switch to PROD
  • TAGGING AND RESOURCE GROUPS – Resource groups make it easier to group your resources using tags. Makes query / lookup by tags easier
  • VPC PEERING: Has to be within a single region – VPC peering is not transitive. VPC peering can be done with different VPCs of the same account OR with VPCs of different account (and as long as they are in the same region)
  • DIRECT CONNECT: VPN connections go through the internet. AWS Direct Connect does not. It is a dedicated / direct (dark fibre) connection between your facility and AWS (i.e. AWS DirectConnect facility)
  • SECURITY TOKEN SERVICE: Ex. employees logging into enterprise reporting application first need to log into the company’s AD instance
  • Identity broker will typically be a custom application. It checks with LDAP/AD first and then checks with AWS STS
  • Application gets temporary access to AWS resources
  • ACTIVE DIRECTORY INTEGRATION: Single Sign On for Management Console access
  • AWS WORKSPACES: Basically, a VDI – cloud based replacement for traditional desktop. Think WINDOWS workspace in the cloud – workspaces are persistent – All data in the D:// drive is backed for a period of 12 hours
  • ELASTIC CONTAINER SERVICE
  • Docker Image: template with instructions for creating a docker container – images can be stored in a registry like DockerHub or AWS ECR or other
  • ECS task definitions: required to run docker containers in Amazon ECS – contains info on 1) what docker images to use 2) how much cpu/memory/etc and 3) containers are linked together in a task
  • ECS Cluster: is a logical grouping containing instances that you can place tasks on
  • ECS / Containers / Images / Tasks / ECR / Task Definitions / ECS Clusters

Miscellaneous (Contd.)›

Overview

  • AWS Levels of support: Basic, Developer, Business, Enterprise
  • Types of virtualization: Para Virtual (PV) and Hardware Virtual Machine (HVM)
  • AWS import/Export: export to Amazon Glacier not supported currently
  • AWS does not support increasing storage on a SQL Server RDS instance types
  • If you want to run a database on EC2 instance type then use EBS for storage
  • Min size for MS SQL Server DB instance with SQL Server Express Edition = 10GB
  • Can I force a failover for RDS instance that has been configured for Multi AZ – YES
  • Reserved instances are available for multi AZ deployments – YES
  • Non root EBS Volumes can be detached without stopping the EC2 instance
  • AWS is PCI DSS 1.0 compliant. But in addition, a delta accreditation is required from a QSA if credit card details are to be stored
  • Underlying hypervisor for EC2: XEN
  • Number of copies Aurora DB stores by default = 6
  • Max response time for a Business Level Premium support: 1 HOUR
  • You get root access for the following 2 AWS Service (EC2 and EMR)
  • When you create a new subnet within a custom VPC by default they communicate with each other across AZs
  • You can select a specific AZ in which to place a DynamoDB table – FALSE (In the RDS you can do this)
  • By definition, a public subnet is one that has at least one route in the routing table that uses an internet gateway
  • For new accounts there is a soft limit of 20 EC2 instances per region. A request to AWS needs to be made if we are to have more than 20 instances
  • If you want to enable VPN connection to your VPC then ensure that a public ip address is assigned to the VPC
  • Subnets can communicate by default with other subnets. Nothing special to do
  • Social engineering security attacks are not detectable by AWS
  • Increase SQS long polling (ReceiveMessageWaitTimeSeconds) to avoid high costs due to frequent polling
  • DynamoDB allows for the storage of large text and binary objects (combined value + name) but there is a limit – 400kb
  • Number of VPCs per region per account = 5
  • VPC Peering enables one VPC to talk to another but VPN/IGs in one VPC will not apply to another
  • During AutoScaling ramp down the oldest launced will go down first
  • The availability, durability, throughput, low latency is the same between S3 – standard and S3 – IA [only cost differs]
  • S3 supports website redirects
  • Across AWS accounts the AZs of EC2 instances are randomized. i.e. If 2 different accounts both pick us-west-2a there is no guarantee that the EC2 instances will reside in the same (i.e. in us-west-2a)
  • 2 ways to restore objects from Glacier 1) using S3 API and 2) using AWS console
  • SLA for EC2 and EBS within a given region 99.95%
  • Minimum size for an object in S3-IA = 128KB
  • For backup files consider S3 – IA over S3 since…
  • Classic ELB supports both IPV4 and IPV6
  • Securely upload to AWS S3 (SSL over HTTPS / HTTP over HTTPS)
  • Move VM for on prem to AWS [VM Import/Export]
  • Elastic IP Addresses limit / region by default = 5
  • By default you can have 100 S3 buckets in a region
  • Cloud Watch trials are stored for a period of 2 weeks
  • S3 cross region replication by default, metadata and ACLs are also copied over
  • When you want tighter controls over the keys use SSE-C instead of SSE-KMS or S3
  • Small DB loads with infrequent access use AWS Magnetic Storage.
HIGH–Provisioned I/O    MED-General Purpose    Low - Magnetic
  • Pillars of well architected framework {Security | Reliability | Performance Efficiency | Cost Optimization | Operational Excellence}
  • If your project gets cancelled what do you do to the reserved instances – sell them in the AWS Marketplace
  • By default, RDS instances are backed up one day at no charge
  • Automated backups of RDS instances are deleted when the instance id deleted. A final snapshot MAY have been created depending whether ‘SkipFinalSnapshot’ parameter was set to ‘False’
  • Oracle/SQL Server do not support read replicas at this point
  • S3 is a global service and loss of one AZ has no effect
  • RDS replicas – asynchronous replication | RDS AZs synchronous replication
  • What is the minimum number of VMs that can be migrated concurrently = 50
  • When you stop an EC2 instance 1) underlying host for the instance will be changed 2) All instance store data is deleted and 3) elastic IP address remains unchanged
  • When EBS volume snapshot is being taken then the EC2 instance cannot communicate with the EBS volumes
  • Hardware VPN Connection – secure conn between your data center and VPC
  • When cross zone load balancing is disables then each AZ receives 50% of the traffic irrespective of the number of EC2 instances in the AZ
  • You typically want to have cross zone LB enabled so load is spread across the EC2 instances evenly
  • One EFS can be attached to multiple EC2 instances and vice versa – One EBS volumes can be attached to only one EC2 instance – But one EC2 instance can be attached to multiple EBS volumes
